page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5i-c2g1.large is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.gn5i server family. It is built on the x86_64 CPU architecture and runs on the KVM hypervisor. The instance features 2 dedicated physical CPU cores with 2 vCPUs, operating with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core.

Acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA P4 GPU with 8 GB of VRAM. The instance does not include local storage, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. Network connectivity is configured with a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource density perspective, this instance represents an entry-level GPU configuration. The combination of a modest dual-core CPU, 8 GB of system memory, and a single P4 GPU makes it a cost-effective option for workloads that require GPU acceleration but do not demand massive parallel compute or memory footprints. Suitable workloads include light GPU-accelerated inference, video transcoding, and basic CUDA-based development or testing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5i-c2g1.large is a GPU-accelerated instance from the ecs.gn5i family, running on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 8.0 GB of system memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. The instance is equipped with a single NVIDIA P4 GPU featuring 8 GB of VRAM, while local storage is absent. This hardware profile positions the instance as a cost-effective entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads that do not require massive memory or CPU resources. It is designed for workloads such as light machine learning inference, video transcoding, and CUDA development.
